purpose: Evidence that make ci no longer leaves the working tree dirty with generated sandbox-profile artifacts, that canonical evidence remains tracked, and that make verify-drift passes against committed state.

drift_source.non_determinism_cause: profile content embeds a per-process random temp path issued by mkdtemp(3) such as /T/osisol-fields-XXXXXXXX and /T/governed-run-XXXXXXXX; each invocation produces new file content and therefore a new content-hash filename

make_verify_drift.stdout_tail: OK: regenerated artifacts match committed state.

known_remaining_hygiene_issues.summary: 225 already-tracked stale profile files remain in the repo
known_remaining_hygiene_issues.detail: These were committed before WO 019. They contain the same non-deterministic per-process temp paths described in drift_source.non_determinism_cause. .gitignore does not untrack already-tracked files. Fix: future work order should run `git rm --cached reports/os_isolation_runtime/profiles/*.sb`.

known_remaining_hygiene_issues.summary: Default PYTHON=python3 in Makefile resolves to system Python without project deps
known_remaining_hygiene_issues.detail: On this machine `python3` is homebrew 3.14.3, which lacks pytest/jsonschema. The pinned deps live in .venv. A fresh-clone contributor running `make ci` fails at the `test` stage with `No module named pytest`. Fix options: (a) Makefile auto-detects .venv/bin/python; (b) README pins the invocation.

known_remaining_hygiene_issues.summary: Rust verifier emits 3 dead-code warnings during every CI run
known_remaining_hygiene_issues.detail: Unused: function `canonical_sha256_hex` (src/jcs.rs:56), function `sha256_prefixed` (src/jcs.rs:71), fields `file`/`protocol_version` on struct `Vector` (src/vectors.rs:47,52). Non-blocking.

what_is_now_fixed: CI no longer leaves untracked profile artifacts in the working tree.
Future CI runs cannot pollute drift checks via the sandbox-profile path.
